title: The War on Poverty's Experiment in Public Medicine: Community Health Centers and the Mortality of Older Americans
notes: Bailey & Goodman-Bacon (2015). AER. Outcome: amr_eld = age-adjusted mortality rate (50+, per 100k), weight=popwt_eld. Protocol rule (i): paper title='Mortality of Older Americans', abstract emphasizes 50+ result, Figure 7D is the key event study. Staggered rollout of Community Health Centers 1965-1974 (early CHCs only; late CHCs 1975-1980 treated as never-treated). 114 treated counties, 2948 control counties. ~91k obs, 3062 counties, 1959-1988. Stata: xtreg amr_eld _Iyear* _IyeaXDu* _IyeaXst* D_* R_* H_* _DD* [aw=popwt_eld], cluster(fips) fe. Table 2 Panel B Col 2 / Figure 7D. FEs: county + year + urban-category×year (Durb = 5 bins of 1960 urban share). Cols 3/6 add state×year (not in baseline). D_ = baseline×trend (Pattern 37), R_/H_ = time-varying. 10 cohorts (1965-1974). SA applicable. Drops NYC/LA/Chicago. CS-NT DIAGNOSTICS (Pattern 51): Baseline CS-NT (xformla=~1) showed +40-50 pre-trend bias vs TWFE≈0. Root cause: identification relies on Durb×year + state×year FEs that CS-NT can't absorb. Tested: (1) xformla=~Durb_f (5 urban categories) → pre-trends reduced ~80% to +10-15, ATT=-59 (SE=20); (2) + state dummies → overshoot to negative pre-trends (Pattern 42 overfitting). Fix: cs_controls=[Durb_f]. 5 urban dummies capture most of the confounding without propensity overfitting. Remaining gap vs TWFE due to state×year + baseline×trend controls that only regression-based estimators absorb.
